Since this is going around again I'll briefly explain what happened. I worked at this silly job in San Jose with @fart where we goofed off a lot. At the same time, I was a writer for the Boston Phoenix and I wrote mostly about stupid music industry stuff.

I used to spend hours every day while I was supposed to be working reading press releases about music, because I often wrote stupid music press release roundups. One day this especially stupid one caught my eye.

This product called SHEETS, which were like Listerine breath strips with caffeine in them, was doing a cross-promo with Walmart and Pitbull where the Walmart store that got the most FB likes would win a personal appearance by Pitbull.

I immediately brought it over to @fart cause I realized we could easily get people to vote for whatever the funniest possible Walmart was. We had google maps open with a list of Walmart locations and everything and we were scouring for the most remote ones.

When we saw there one in Kodiak we both bust out laughing because not only was it way up in Alaska, it was on this tiny island BELOW Alaska, which was also named after huge fucking bears, so we knew that was definitely the one.

I think what happened after this was since @fart had a big following on Twitter we decided we'd start spreading the word there. At the same time I think I wrote about it in the Phoenix and described what we were doing and whatnot.

Then pretty soon it not only got seized on by "the internet" at large (like 4chan or Reddit if that was even a thing at the time), but also by a bunch of media outlets who thought it was funny and wrote about it.

Walmart started freaking out about it and the SHEETS PR people were getting in touch and stuff. I can't remember how it went down exactly, but eventually Pitbull's management called me and said they thought it was hilarious and they were all in.

Pitbull's people said Walmart warned them I was "anti-establishment" and they were like "great, we're anti-establishment too, let's do this." So they found the ONE DAY OFF Pitbull had on his tour and booked a trip to Kodiak.

Pitbull also flew me first-class to Alaska, which was a very nice thing to do, and I wrote a big thing about it for The Phoenix which may still exist somewhere. Pitbull and his people were incredibly cool and in on the joke.
that's probably true but the funniness of the situation works just as well either way
